title: Defense of Conscience in Health Care Act
short_title: Defense of Conscience in Health Care Act
stage_label: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
plain_summary: This bill tells the Department of Health and Human Services to create new rules that protect healthcare workers who don't want to do certain medical procedures because of their religious or personal beliefs. The new rules must be exactly like older rules from 2019 that were supposed to protect these workers but were thrown out by courts before they could be used. The 2019 rules had stronger punishments for hospitals or clinics that forced workers to do things against their beliefs. Healthcare workers would be protected if they refused to do things like provide abortions or refer patients for abortions. The government has six months to put these protective rules back in place.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
subjects: Department of Health and Human Services

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Defense of Conscience in Health Care Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Regulations on Protecting Statutory Conscience Rights in Health Care
sections.text: Within six months after this law is passed, the Secretary of Health and Human Services must do two things. First, the Secretary must create a final rule under federal laws that protect conscience rights and prevent discrimination. This rule must be the same as or very similar to an old rule that was part eighty-eight of title forty-five in the Code of Federal Regulations. That old rule was active on July twenty-second, two thousand nineteen and was about protecting conscience rights that are written into law for health care workers. Second, the Secretary must state in this new final rule that it replaces any conflicting rules that exist when the new rule is published. In this section, federal conscience and anti-discrimination laws means the same thing it meant in the old rule mentioned above.
